question: A company sells a board game. According to the chart, which statement about the latest year is appropriate?

options.A: The latest year proves that copies sold is now permanently declining.
options.B: From this chart, we cannot make a reliable conclusion about the latest full-year trend because the year is only partial.
options.C: The latest year clearly has the lowest copies sold of the entire period.
options.D: The latest year shows a sharp decline in annual sales compared to previous years.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 3 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (578 copies) directly to full-year values like 2,464 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
